content:
It seems that KERN_CONT is heavily discouraged: the comment in kern_levels.h says:

Only to be used by core/arch code during early bootup (a continued line is not SMP-safe otherwise). Maybe we shouldn't expose it in Rust at all?

With powerful formatting capability of core::fmt I don't see it being very useful anyway.

Agreed, even checkpatch.pl warns about it. And yet there are ~1k uses inside drivers/... :/ When I wrote this I was trying to produce the same functionality, more or less. But yeah, removing would simplify a few conditionals and one macro case.

Perhaps we can put it as a good first issue (asking, nevertheless, to sample a few of those C drivers to see the use cases in case we miss something).
